title: Managed PostgreSQL on Supabase
description: Supabase managed PostgreSQL with bundled Auth, Storage, and Realtime subscriptions. Free tier (500 MB database, 1 GB storage, 50K MAU) suits prototypes and MVPs; validate free-tier constraints (pausing, connection limits) before committing to production.

cost.costNotes: Free tier: 500MB database, 1GB file storage, 2GB bandwidth, 50K MAU. Projects pause after 1 week of inactivity; upgrade to Pro ($25/month) for always-on and increased limits.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: This pattern specifically targets the Supabase free tier which provides $0/month service cost. The free tier includes 500MB database, 1GB file storage, 2GB bandwidth, and 50K monthly active users. Min represents a small prototype or MVP staying within free tier limits. Max is also $0 because this pattern is explicitly designed for free-tier usage. Note: Projects pause after 1 week of inactivity on free tier. If usage exceeds free tier limits or always-on availability is needed, the Pro plan at $25/month would be required, but that would be a different pattern (not free-tier-first).
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-low complexity implementation. Supabase provides well-documented SDKs for JavaScript/TypeScript with straightforward APIs for database, authentication, storage, and realtime features. With modern AI coding assistants, developers can quickly integrate Supabase client libraries. Implementation involves: setting up Supabase project (30 mins), configuring auth flows (2-3 hours), implementing database queries with PostgreSQL (3-4 hours), setting up file storage (1-2 hours), and integrating realtime subscriptions if needed (2-3 hours). Total ~12-15 hours at $50-75/hour developer rate with AI assistance. The learning curve is gentle due to excellent documentation and similarity to Firebase. Lower than custom backend development but requires understanding of Supabase-specific patterns and PostgreSQL.
provenance.licenseCost: Supabase free tier has no license cost. The platform is open-source (Apache 2.0 and MIT licenses) and can be self-hosted, but this pattern uses the hosted free tier service. No commercial licenses, seats, or upfront fees required. The free tier includes all core features: PostgreSQL database, authentication (email, social providers), storage, realtime subscriptions, and Edge Functions (limited invocations). Paid features and higher limits require upgrading to Pro ($25/month) or Team/Enterprise tiers, but those are runtime costs, not license costs.

provides.reasoning: Supabase free tier provides a fully managed PostgreSQL database with 500MB storage included, handling database infrastructure, backups, and maintenance without requiring user management of the underlying database server.
Supabase Auth is a core feature providing user authentication and session management with support for multiple providers and JWT-based sessions.
Supabase free tier includes 1GB of S3-compatible object storage through its Storage API, allowing applications to store and serve files with built-in access controls and CDN delivery.
Supabase Realtime enables WebSocket-based subscriptions to database changes, allowing clients to receive live updates when data changes occur.
Supabase auto-generates RESTful APIs from PostgreSQL schemas using PostgREST, providing instant API access to database tables without manual endpoint creation.
This pattern fundamentally provides a complete BaaS solution combining database, authentication, storage, and APIs in a unified platform for rapid application development.
Supabase includes PostgreSQL Row Level Security (RLS) for fine-grained access control, allowing developers to define authorization policies directly at the database level.
Supabase exposes PostgreSQL's native RLS with a first-class UI policy editor and auth.uid() helper, making per-tenant row isolation a first-class feature.

Supabase Auth issues JWTs with user/tenant claims that are available inside PostgreSQL RLS policies via auth.uid() and custom JWT claims, enabling tenant-aware query enforcement at the database layer.

requires.reasoning: As a SaaS platform, this pattern requires internet connectivity to access Supabase's hosted services and infrastructure.
This pattern requires a client application (web, mobile, or desktop) to consume the Supabase services, as it provides backend infrastructure that must be accessed by frontend applications.

supports_nfr.reason: Free tier lacks enterprise SLA guarantees and dedicated resources; maximum practical availability is 99%.
Free-tier shared infrastructure and potential cold starts contribute to minimum p95 latency of 100ms.
Free-tier shared resources and cold starts contribute to p99 latency floor of 200ms.
Free tier automated failover and service recovery typically completes within 10-15 minutes minimum.
Free tier PostgreSQL uses write-ahead logging with 15-minute backup intervals for point-in-time recovery.
Free tier bandwidth (2GB/month) and resource constraints limit sustained peak throughput to approximately 50K jobs/hour.
Free tier 500MB database and shared connection pool limit read query throughput to approximately 100 QPS.
Free tier write throughput constrained by single PostgreSQL instance and limited resources to approximately 50 QPS.
Supabase provides built-in OAuth2/OIDC and password auth, but can be paired with any auth mechanism (JWT, mTLS, API key) or none specified (n/a) at the application layer.
Supabase provides basic audit logging through PostgreSQL logs and auth event tracking, optional for applications.
Free tier supports PostgreSQL row-level security (RLS) for shared database isolation or schema-per-tenant patterns.
PII with compliance requirements may require paid plans with BAA/compliance agreements not available in free tier.
Supabase supports GDPR compliance features but free tier may lack full compliance guarantees for production use.
Free tier does not include HIPAA BAA; HIPAA compliance requires paid tier with signed agreements.
Supabase provides data access and deletion capabilities for CCPA but free tier lacks compliance SLAs.
Free tier lacks audit trail guarantees and retention policies required for SOX compliance in production.
PostgreSQL with synchronous replication provides read-your-writes consistency by default for single-region deployments.
Free tier does not guarantee strict durability; backup retention is limited to 7 days with best-effort recovery.

supports_constraints.reason: Supabase is a cloud-agnostic SaaS platform accessible from any environment with internet connectivity.
Supabase provides official SDKs for JavaScript/TypeScript, Python, Dart (Flutter), Go, Kotlin, Swift, C#, and Rust.
Supabase is a backend-as-a-service that supports web applications, mobile apps (iOS/Android), API backends, and CLI tools.
Pattern specifically requires Supabase as the backend provider
Free-tier caching can be implemented with external services or application-level caching for small-scale needs.
Basic async messaging can work on free tiers but high-volume message queues typically require paid services.
Some free-tier AI providers (Hugging Face, limited OpenAI credits) support prototype AI inference alongside Supabase.
Multi-tenancy is supported at small scale on free tiers, but row-level security and tenant isolation require careful design.
Supabase free tier does not include serverless functions or batch processing capabilities natively.
Single PostgreSQL instance; no distributed transaction coordination across services in free tier.
Supabase includes built-in Realtime subscriptions for live data streaming via WebSockets.
Supabase supports pgvector extension for vector similarity search and AI embeddings on free tier.
Supabase PostgreSQL supports JSONB for document-oriented data patterns alongside relational tables.
Free tier does not include dedicated key-value store; use PostgreSQL tables instead for simple key-value needs.
Supabase does not provide native graph database capabilities; use dedicated graph database for relationship-heavy queries.
Free tier PostgreSQL not optimized for high-volume time-series workloads; use dedicated time-series database instead.
Supabase PostgreSQL is optimized for OLTP workloads with transactional consistency and row-level operations.
Free tier 500MB database unsuitable for analytical workloads; use dedicated OLAP database or data warehouse.

auth_providers.description: Authentication strategy: email (password-based), email-social (add OAuth providers like Google/GitHub), magic-link (passwordless), or phone (SMS-based). Social and phone may have additional costs beyond free tier.

storage_enabled.description: Enable file storage (1GB free tier). Disable if only using database to simplify architecture.

realtime_enabled.description: Enable realtime subscriptions for live data updates. Disable for simpler request-response patterns to reduce complexity and bandwidth usage.

row_level_security.description: Enable PostgreSQL Row Level Security for database access control. Recommended for production but can be disabled for rapid prototyping. Critical for multi-tenant applications.

warn_nfr.reason: Free tier is constrained to ~100 QPS read throughput; approaching this limit risks throttling and performance degradation.
warn_nfr.message: db-managed-postgres--supabase: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (>80). Free tier supports up to ~100 QPS; consider upgrading to paid tier for headroom.

warn_nfr.reason: Free tier is constrained to ~50 QPS write throughput; approaching this limit risks throttling and write latency spikes.
warn_nfr.message: db-managed-postgres--supabase: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (>40). Free tier supports up to ~50 QPS; consider upgrading to paid tier for write-heavy workloads.
